channel.title: #DigitalBlackOutIran
channel.description: Daily simulation of how much GDP the world and the top 10 economies would lose if their own internet were blacked out for as many days as Iran's.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 33
item.description: 2026-04-01 internet blackout simulation: day 33, world scenario $51.07B/day. United States leads at $14.93B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 22
item.description: Day 22 simulation: world scenario $45.66B/day; biggest country case: United States $13.35B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 21
item.description: Day 21 simulation: world scenario $45.12B/day; biggest country case: United States $13.19B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 20
item.description: Day 20 simulation: world scenario $44.57B/day; biggest country case: United States $13.03B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 19
item.description: Day 19 simulation: world scenario $44.00B/day; biggest country case: United States $12.86B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 18
item.description: Day 18 simulation: world scenario $43.42B/day; biggest country case: United States $12.69B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 17
item.description: Day 17 simulation: world scenario $42.83B/day; biggest country case: United States $12.52B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 16
item.description: Day 16 simulation: world scenario $42.22B/day; biggest country case: United States $12.34B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 15
item.description: Day 15 simulation: world scenario $41.58B/day; biggest country case: United States $12.16B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 14
item.description: Day 14 simulation: world scenario $40.93B/day; biggest country case: United States $11.96B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 13
item.description: Day 13 simulation: world scenario $40.25B/day; biggest country case: United States $11.77B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 12
item.description: Day 12 simulation: world scenario $39.54B/day; biggest country case: United States $11.56B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 11
item.description: 2026-03-10 update: simulated internet blackout losses put United States first, China second, with world exposure at $38.79B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 10
item.description: Day 10 economic impact simulation: if internet went dark locally, United States would lose $11.11B/day; world $38.00B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 9
item.description: 2026-03-08 internet blackout simulation: day 9, world scenario $37.16B/day. United States leads at $10.86B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 8
item.description: Internet outage simulation day 8: world daily loss $36.26B, cumulative world loss $257.31B; United States remains the largest economy case.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 7
item.description: 2026-03-06 blackout simulation archive: United States, China and Germany top the loss table; world scenario totals $35.28B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 6
item.description: Day 6 blackout simulation report: United States $10.08B/day, China $5.41B/day, world $34.49B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 5
item.description: SEO update for 2026-03-04: simulated blackout losses show United States at $9.81B/day and world exposure at $33.57B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 4
item.description: Internet blackout day 4: modeled daily loss reaches $32.43B worldwide, with United States and China leading the country ranking.
